d: Double Balancier Convexe in carbon brings Greubel Forsey’s modern architecture into a darker, more technical register. Its twin inclined balance wheels create both visual drama and chronometric purpose, while the lightweight case sharpens the watch’s already aggressive stance. It feels futuristic, mechanical, and unapologetically sculptural, a high-concept expression of contemporary independent watchmaking.

Double Balancier Convexe in titanium gives one of Greubel Forsey’s most dynamic creations a brighter, more architectural presence. The twin balance system is not merely a technical flourish, but the visual heart of the watch, creating depth, symmetry, and constant motion on the wrist. Bold yet remarkably resolved, it captures the manufacture’s ability to turn chronometric invention into design theatre.

Balancier Convexe S² in titanium refines the Convexe idea into something slightly more compact, yet no less intense. Its curved case, open architecture, and suspended balance create a sense of mechanical tension that feels both highly technical and beautifully controlled. It is a more distilled take on the Greubel Forsey language, still radical, but especially wearable.

Balancier Convexe S² in carbon pushes Greubel Forsey’s contemporary design language into an even stealthier and more experimental direction. The lightweight case amplifies the watch’s angular energy, while the openworked dial keeps the balance at the centre of the experience. It feels sharp, modern, and purpose-built, a statement piece with genuine horological substance.

Tourbillon Cardan is one of Greubel Forsey’s most spectacular demonstrations of mechanical choreography, using a gimbal-style construction to give the regulating organ a constant sense of motion in space. Yet for all its complexity, the watch remains remarkably coherent, every bridge and surface contributing to a unified visual rhythm. It is inventive watchmaking elevated into moving sculpture.

QP Balancier brings the perpetual calendar into the Greubel Forsey universe with unusual clarity and composure. Rather than overwhelming the dial, its indications are integrated into an architecture that still feels open, balanced, and deeply modern. The result is a perpetual calendar that is not only technically impressive, but visually alive in a way few examples of the complication ever achieve.

Nano Foudroyante compresses extraordinary technical ambition into a watch that remains surprisingly refined in scale and presentation. Its high-frequency display adds a flicker of living energy to the dial, turning precision into something immediately visible and emotionally engaging. Compact, innovative, and highly distinctive, it proves that Greubel Forsey can be no less daring in smaller form.

Hand Made 2 continues Greubel Forsey’s radical commitment to artisanal watchmaking, where the overwhelming majority of components are created and finished by hand. The beauty of the watch lies not only in its mechanics, but in the visible human labour embedded in every surface and contour. It feels less like industrial production and more like horology returned to its purest origins.

Hand Made 1 stands as one of the most uncompromising statements in modern horology, reviving an almost vanished tradition of constructing a watch almost entirely by hand. Every detail carries the weight of time, patience, and human skill, giving the piece an emotional depth beyond its mechanical function. It is not simply rare watchmaking, but watchmaking as cultural preservation.

Grande Sonnerie brings one of horology’s most demanding acoustic complications into the Greubel Forsey world with striking authority. The watch balances visual restraint and technical gravity, allowing its chiming mechanism to remain the true source of its presence. It is an object of immense complexity, yet what comes through most strongly is the discipline and seriousness behind its execution.

Balancier Contemporain distils Greubel Forsey’s philosophy into a smaller and more intimate format, without losing any of the brand’s signature depth or finish. Its open architecture, prominent balance, and layered case profile create the same sense of mechanical sculpture found in larger pieces, only in a more concentrated form. It is thoughtful, refined, and quietly radical.

Tourbillon 24 Secondes Architecture turns Greubel Forsey’s signature fast-rotating tourbillon into a full spatial composition, where bridges, volumes, and inclined planes shape the entire identity of the watch. The result is less like a conventional dial and more like a mechanical landscape. Monumental, sculptural, and deeply technical, it captures the manufacture at its most theatrical.

GMT Balancier Convexe brings travel time into the Collection Convexe with the same muscular architecture and strong visual depth that define the line. Its globe display and prominent balance create a constant sense of motion, while the convex case gives the watch a uniquely modern wrist presence. It is practical in function, but unmistakably dramatic in execution.

Double Balancier Convexe presents one of Greubel Forsey’s most recognisable technical ideas in a form that feels especially modern and assertive. The twin balance system gives the watch its visual and conceptual centre, while the convex case sharpens the entire composition into something angular and alive. It is bold, complex, and instantly identifiable.

Balancier Convexe S² refines the Greubel Forsey Convexe formula into a more compact and concentrated expression. Its curved case, deep architecture, and highly visible balance create an immediate sense of mechanical tension, yet the overall watch remains remarkably coherent. It feels like a distilled version of the brand’s contemporary language, every detail sharpened, nothing diluted.

Balancier 3 explores chronometric performance through an unusually sculptural display, giving the regulating system a presence that is both technical and deeply visual. Within Collection Convexe, it feels like a natural continuation of Greubel Forsey’s pursuit of mechanical innovation through form. It is precise, architectural, and unmistakably independent in spirit.
